lede: A look at August 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Michael Jackson and John Locke, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Vnukovo Airlines Flight 2801
events.desc: Vnukovo Airlines Flight 2801, a Tupolev Tu-154, crashes into a mountain on the Arctic island of Spitsbergen, killing all 141 aboard.

events.title: Libero Grassi
events.desc: Libero Grassi, an Italian businessman from Palermo, is killed by the Sicilian Mafia after taking a solitary stand against their extortion demands.

events.title: Air France Flight 343
events.desc: Air France Flight 343 crashes on approach to Yoff Airport in Senegal, killing all 63 aboard.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Start of the Battle of St. Quentin in which the French Fifth Army counter-attacked the invading Germans at Saint-Quentin, Aisne.

events.title: Russian battleship Slava
events.desc: The Slava, the last of the five Borodino-class battleships, is launched.

events.title: Gottlieb Daimler
events.desc: Gottlieb Daimler patents the world's first motorcycle with an internal combustion engine, the Reitwagen.

events.title: Treaty of Nanking
events.desc: Treaty of Nanking signing ends the First Opium War.

events.title: Nuuk
events.desc: The city of Nuuk in Greenland is founded as the fort of Godt-Haab by the royal governor Claus Paarss.

events.title: Hundred Years' War
events.desc: Hundred Years' War: The Treaty of Picquigny formally ends the war between the kingdoms of France and England.

births.name: Michael Jackson
births.line: American singer-songwriter, producer, dancer, and actor

births.name: Maurice Maeterlinck
births.line: Belgian poet and playwright, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Beheading of St. John the Baptist
Christian feast day: Euphrasia Eluvathingal (Syro-Malabar Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Vitalis, Sator and Repositus
